title: verbascum pterocalycinum
intro: Verbascum pterocalycinum, also known as Greek mullein, is a biennial or perennial plant species within the Verbascum genus, characterized by its tall spikes of white or pink flowers and thick, fuzzy leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Verbascum pterocalycinum include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and combat bacterial and fungal infections. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and is often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds, or taken internally to support respiratory health.
